grade_basis: morning-of-case surgical usefulness for right parasagittal 4cm meningioma abutting SSS
overall_usefulness: operatively useful with correct venous-preservation framing, but limited by missing patient-specific imaging, placeholders, and non-quantitative evidence extraction
evidence_usefulness: good bottom-line synthesis; directly supports abutment-vs-invasion caution, MRV/CTV confirmation, bridging/cortical vein preservation, and planned residual when venous risk is high
evidence_effect: positive: converts sources into an actionable venous-preservation strategy rather than a raw bibliography
top_defects: No patient-specific imaging details: AP location, SSS patency/narrowing, actual abutment vs invasion, venous lacunae, and dominant bridging veins remain unknown.
Multiple generic placeholders remain in case summary, approach matrix, checklists, symptoms/exam, anticoagulation, consent, and team questions.
Operative plan is directionally correct but lacks concrete incision/craniotomy/burr-hole/dural-opening strategy around the sinus and bridging veins.
Immediate SSS bleeding rescue logic is correct but too high-level; lacks practical repair/packing sequence and air embolism/venous pressure contingencies.
Evidence section cites relevant studies but lacks extracted numeric outcomes or concrete study-level takeaways for morbidity, recurrence, venous infarct, sinus thrombosis, or radiosurgery control.
Evidence provenance is internally inconsistent: README/appendix report one source while evidence tables list multiple curated sources.
strengths: Does not overcall SSS invasion from 'abutting SSS'.
Centers MRV/CTV-defined sinus and venous anatomy before deciding extent of resection.
Correctly prioritizes cortical/bridging vein preservation and planned residual over unsafe radicality.
Includes relevant stop points for unclear sinus plane, dominant vein compromise, venous congestion, and uncontrolled venous bleeding.
Avoids irrelevant mandatory MEP/SSEP and overbuilt Sindou-style default management.
